summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the unauthenticated /payment/api/editable/update endpoint accepts arbitrary PHP content, writes it to an existing payment listener file, and the injected code executes when the file is loaded, demonstrating remote code execution.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should cause the crafted PHP payload to be written to after_pay.php via the unauthenticated API endpoint, and the injected code should execute and create a proof marker file, confirming arbitrary code execution in the application context.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the Docker image, created a fresh Laravel 10 application with pay-uz installed, published the editable payment files, sent an unauthenticated GET request to /payment/api/editable/update with a malicious PHP payload as the content parameter. The API returned {"responseStatus":"success","message":"Muvaffaqiyatli saqlandi"}. The file on disk was confirmed to contain the attacker-injected PHP payload. Executing the modified file produced the proof marker /tmp/evohunt_rce_proof containing EVOHUNT_RCE_CONFIRMED.
verification_method: The verification script checks three independent signals: (1) the API endpoint returns a JSON response containing 'success' confirming the file write was accepted, (2) the file on disk contains the attacker-controlled PHP payload confirming the write persisted, and (3) executing the modified PHP file produces a proof marker file at a known path with a known value, distinguishing real code execution from a generic error or startup failure.
evidence_produced_by_run: The /payment/api/editable/update endpoint returned a JSON success response, confirming the unauthenticated file write was processed.
The after_pay.php file on disk was verified to contain the attacker-injected PHP payload after the API call.
Executing the modified after_pay.php created a proof marker file at /tmp/evohunt_rce_proof containing EVOHUNT_RCE_CONFIRMED, confirming arbitrary PHP code execution.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable ref, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
